task_completion.description: Log in to the Odoo application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Odoo web interface is visible in the browser, indicating a successful login (e.g., the main app dashboard is shown).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Quality Checks section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the Odoo Quality module, specifically the Quality Checks list or kanban view.

task_completion.description: Open the pending Quality Check for the 'Office Chair'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The detailed form view for a Quality Check is open, and the Product field clearly displays 'Office Chair'.

task_completion.description: Upload the picture evidence from the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file upload dialog shows '/home/ga/Documents/chair_proof.jpg' being selected, or the uploaded image (a gray background with text) is visible in the picture field of the Quality Check form.
The exact text 'Visual verification complete' is typed into the note field, instructions tab, or the chatter/log section of the Quality Check.

task_completion.description: Mark the Quality Check as Passed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Pass' button is clicked, and the Quality Check form displays a 'Passed' status indicator (usually a green banner or status badge at the top right).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Odoo web interface via a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser (like Chromium/Firefox) navigating the Odoo UI, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or Python scripts to use the XML-RPC API.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely uploaded the image file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file picker dialog was used to select the file from the local filesystem, or a drag-and-drop action was performed, rather than just typing the file path into a random text field.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the final state.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) being open to manually alter the DOM text to say 'Passed' or 'Visual verification complete'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target product is exactly 'Office Chair'. The proof file is located at '/home/ga/Documents/chair_proof.jpg' (it is an 800x600 JPEG image). The expected note is exactly 'Visual verification complete' (case-insensitive). The final expected status of the Quality Check is 'Passed' (internal state 'pass').
